stem: A urban planning study measures the commute time (minutes) for a sample of 50 city workers. The sample mean is 150.0 min with a sample standard deviation of 38.9 min. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 150.0 min. 95% CI = [138.9, 161.1]. Point estimate: 150.0 min.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
